name: claude_code_task
description: Run Claude Code as an autonomous coding agent on THIS machine, in the operator's shell with FULL permissions - it can read and write files and run any command in the working directory. Asynchronous: it returns a task ID immediately and runs in the background — the user can watch its progress on the live task pill; check get_task_status if asked whether it's done, and cancel it with the task cancel endpoint. Use it for real multi-step coding and system tasks in a working directory, not for quick questions.

prompt.description: The task for the coding agent, written in plain language (e.g. 'add a /health endpoint to app.py and run the test suite').

model.description: Optional. The model CLASS to run: one of 'opus', 'sonnet', 'haiku', or 'fable'. Claude Code resolves the class to its newest version for you. Omit to use the CLI's own default model.

cwd.description: Optional. Absolute path of a real project directory to run the agent in (e.g. a repo you want it to work on). Omit to run in an isolated per-task scratch workspace created just for this run.

returns: task_id (string) for async status tracking via get_task_status
example: claude_code_task(prompt="add tests for the parser and run them")
